name: Model Context Protocol + MCP Registry
description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open source project that welcomes contributions from the community

name: mcp-scan
description: Detects tool poisoning, rug pulls (hash-based tool pinning), and prompt injection across Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Windsurf. uvx mcp-scan@latest, no config, free.

name: OWASP GenAI Security Project
description: Global, open-source initiative dedicated to identifying, mitigating, and documenting security and safety risks associated with generative AI technologies, including large language models (LLMs), agentic AI systems, and AI-driven applications

name: mcp-use
description: Open-source SDK (formerly mcp-use) to connect any LLM to any MCP server in a few lines of code, plus hosted deployment for MCP servers and apps. Not a security tool itself — it's the build/deploy layer you harden with the scanners, gateways, and auth controls listed here.

name: agentgateway
description: Open-source data-plane gateway for HTTP, LLM, MCP, and A2A traffic. MCP control plane with RBAC, tool scoping, OPA policy, audit trails, and PII redaction. Donated to the Agentic AI Foundation.

name: Lunar.dev MCPX
description: MCP-focused observability, rate limiting, and compliance. Lightweight visibility into what your agents are actually calling.

name: Backslash Security
description: MCP scanning, hardening, and a real-time MCP proxy built for IDE / vibe-coding workflows. Relevant if your dev loop is agent-heavy.

name: Golf Scanner
description: Discover and audit MCP servers across your IDEs.

name: mcp-sec-audit
description: Research artifact for detecting security vulnerabilities in Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers

name: Lasso Security
description: MCP threat intelligence, red-teaming tooling, and agentic security research. Free-tier scanning and research resources make it a useful reference for solo devs hardening their setups.

name: Operant AI
description: MCP gateway w/ runtime protection + NHI security; free 2026 Guide to Securing MCP and Shadow Escape research.

name: Unified Context Layer (UCL)
description: Emerging open context/MCP gateway layer for agentic systems; useful as your local setup grows past a single client.

name: arcade-mcp
description: Open-source Python framework for building custom, secure MCP servers. Natively handles user-delegated OAuth, token refresh, and encrypted secret injection, so developers never hardcode keys or expose tokens to the client/LLM.
